issue_title: Poor contrast/legibility in message lists, items look greyed out/deactivated

reasoning_trace.finding: The first search using rg failed because rg is not installed; switched to PowerShell Select-String for local-only static analysis.
Confirmed the repo is Thunderbird for Android/K-9 Mail with shared code and white-label apps.
Confirmed the project uses Kotlin, Compose for new UI, existing architecture, and local verification conventions; no external sources were needed.
Found message list feature modules, legacy message list files, and app feature flags controlling message-list implementations.
K-9 release has MessageListFeatureFlags.UseComposeForMessageListItems disabled and EnableMessageListNewState disabled, routing release users to legacy message-list UI.
Thunderbird release uses the same disabled flags for Compose list items and new message-list state, matching the report's Thunderbird app context.
Located newer Compose message-list API/internal components, but release flags indicate they are not the primary path for the reported version.
Located legacy UI implementation files: LegacyMessageListFragment, MessageListAdapter, MessageViewHolder, MessageViewHolderColors, and message_list_item.xml.
MessageListAdapter resolves MessageViewHolderColors once, creates MessageViewHolder when UseComposeForMessageListItems is disabled, and binds MessageListItem rows through MessageViewHolder.bind().
MessageListAppearance includes read-background behavior and display preferences, but no text color values.
The row XML defines subject, preview, date, thread count, star, and icons; subject and preview main colors are assigned dynamically by code, not fixed in XML.
This is only the sealed RecyclerView ViewHolder base class; no color or binding logic.
MessageViewHolder.bind() computes foregroundColor via selectForegroundColor(isSelected, isRead, isActive) and applies it to preview, subject, date, star, attachment, and status icon.
For ordinary read rows, selected and active are false, so the read branch returns colors.read.
MessageViewHolderColors.resolveColors() sets read to Material colorOutline, previewText to colorOutline, and unread to colorOnSurface.

reasoning_trace.target: Read message rows are greyed out because read foreground color is resolved to Material colorOutline
reasoning_trace.finding: The report specifically says mailboxes filled with read emails look greyed out/deactivated; colorOutline is a low-emphasis outline/secondary color and is applied to the main visible content for read rows.
The deprecated Compose row bridge chooses ReadMessageItem for item.isRead and UnreadMessageItem otherwise, but this path is only used when UseComposeForMessageListItems is enabled.
Compose read rows use MessageItemDefaults.defaultMessageItemColors() unless colorizeBackgroundWhenRead is enabled, so read rows are not forced to outline color in the normal Compose path.

defaultMessageItemColors uses MainTheme.colors.onSurface for content; readMessageItemColors uses onSurfaceVariant only when read-background colorization is requested. This eliminates the normal Compose component as the release fault path.
MessageListLauncher launches MessageHomeActivity, identifying the mailbox list entry point used from the app shell.
MessageHomeActivity is declared as the message-list activity handling k9mail://messages links.
MessageHomeActivity is the main activity and obtains MessageListFragmentBridgeContract.Factory to create the mailbox list fragment.
The fragment factory returns LegacyMessageListFragment.Factory when EnableMessageListNewState is disabled; release flags set it disabled.
The bridge contract documents that the factory chooses modern MessageListFragment or LegacyMessageListFragment based on EnableMessageListNewState.
LegacyMessageListFragment creates MessageListAdapter in onCreate(), observes message list LiveData, and assigns loaded rows to adapter.viewItems.
The adapter is built with activity theme and resources, so MessageViewHolderColors.resolveColors(requireActivity().theme) supplies the row colors.
MessageListLoader loads messages from MessageListRepository and maps them through MessageListItemMapper.
MessageListItemMapper copies message.isRead directly into MessageListItem.isRead, so read rows are correctly identified and the symptom is not caused by inverted state mapping.
MessageListItem stores isRead as a Boolean that drives MessageViewHolder color selection.
XML defaults are overridden for subject, preview, date, attachment, star, and status in MessageViewHolder.bind(), so the XML is not the main fault statement.
The mapping preserves the storage read flag and matches the report trigger of read emails; it does not explain low contrast by itself.
Release flags disable the Compose row path, and the normal Compose default uses onSurface for content rather than outline, so it is not the most likely release fault.

This single statement assigns the low-emphasis outline color to the foreground color used for all main text and icons in read legacy message-list rows, directly causing the greyed-out low-contrast appearance.

fault_candidates.reasoning: Release builds route mailbox rows through the legacy MessageViewHolder. For read messages, MessageViewHolder.selectForegroundColor() returns colors.read, and bind() applies that color to the row's subject/sender, preview prefix, date, star, attachment, and status icon. This statement defines colors.read as Material colorOutline, a low-emphasis grey intended for outlines rather than primary list content, which matches the reported greyed-out/deactivated read message rows.

final_answer.causal_chain: K-9/Thunderbird release feature flags disable the new message-list state and Compose list items. MessageHomeActivity therefore creates LegacyMessageListFragment, which creates MessageListAdapter. MessageListAdapter creates legacy MessageViewHolder instances and supplies MessageViewHolderColors.resolveColors(theme). MessageListLoader and MessageListItemMapper preserve message.isRead into MessageListItem.isRead. For a mailbox of read emails, MessageViewHolder.bind() calls selectForegroundColor(false, true, false), which returns colors.read. Because resolveColors() assigned colors.read from MaterialR.attr.colorOutline, the main row foreground becomes a low-emphasis outline grey and is applied to the row's visible text and icons, producing the reported poor contrast and deactivated appearance.
final_answer.fix_hint: Use a higher-emphasis foreground color for read row primary content, such as colorOnSurface or colorOnSurfaceVariant, instead of Material colorOutline.
